parameters.command: =cd /Users/carlos/Brain && (echo '=== CLAUDE.md ===' && cat CLAUDE.md ; echo '=== INBOX (all) ===' && find inbox -type f -name '*.md' -exec echo '--- {} ---' \; -exec cat {} \; ; echo '=== NOTES (last 7d) ===' && find notes -type f -name '*.md' -mtime -7 -exec echo '--- {} ---' \; -exec cat {} \; ; echo '=== IDEAS (all) ===' && find ideas -type f -name '*.md' -exec echo '--- {} ---' \; -exec cat {} \; ; echo '=== PROJECTS ===' && find projects -type f -name '*.md' -exec echo '--- {} ---' \; -exec cat {} \; ; echo '=== RECENT BRIEFS ===' && find daily-briefs -type f -name '*.md' -mtime -7 -exec echo '--- {} ---' \; -exec cat {} \;)

parameters.jsonBody:
={
  "model": "claude-opus-4-7",
  "max_tokens": 6000,
  "system": "You are doing a weekly synthesis pass over Carlos's full Brain vault. Be direct. Challenge him. Do not summarize what he already knows. Output ONLY valid markdown — no preamble, no code fences around the whole thing.",
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": "Read my entire vault below. Focus on everything added in the last 7 days, but draw from older material when it bears on the recent stuff. I want four sections:

1. EMERGING THESIS — what idea am I building toward without having stated it explicitly yet? What position is forming in my thinking?
2. CONTRADICTIONS — what have I saved recently that contradicts something I believed before? Show me both sides from my own notes, with filenames.
3. KNOWLEDGE GAPS — based on what I am reading, what am I clearly NOT reading that I should be? What perspective is missing?
4. ONE ACTION — given everything in the vault, what is the single highest-leverage thing I could do or think about this week?

Format as:
# Weekly synthesis — week of {{ $now.format('yyyy-MM-dd') }}
## Emerging thesis
## Contradictions
## Knowledge gaps
## One action

VAULT:

{{ $json.stdout }}"
    }
  ]
}
